message.content:
- Apple is diversifying its manufacturing and assembly operations outside of China to reduce reliance on the country.
- The company is focusing on India for iPhone and accessory production, Vietnam for AirPods and Mac assembly, Malaysia for some Mac production, and Ireland for simpler products.
- Apple has assembled "tiger teams" to address weaknesses in its supply chain and improve forecasts to anticipate potential shortages.
- The company is also exploring ways to reduce its reliance on Taiwan for chipmaking.
- Apple is moving carefully to maintain a delicate balance within the company and avoid retaliation from China. 
- The company aims to produce 10 million iPhones in India in 2023 and could move as much as 25% of iPhone production to India by 2025 if it sticks to the most aggressive timeline.
- Apple has discussed moving the majority of aluminum iPhone production out of China and has relocated a top iPhone enclosure-quality executive to India.
- The company is looking to build a wider range of components and products in India.
